members.name: swarm-s1
members.spawn_prompt:
## Swarm Task: Inventory: current product feature & code scan

**Task ID:** s1
**Title:** Inventory: current product feature & code scan

Perform a thorough automatic and manual scan of the current product repository (assume working directory is the product root). Produce a machine-readable feature inventory and supporting evidence:

- Output a JSON file at ./artifacts/product_features.json containing an array of discovered features. Each feature entry must include: id, short name, one-sentence description, affected components (frontend/backend/cli/agent/etc.), evidence (file paths, README lines, function/class names, config keys), and confidence level (high/medium/low).
- Create a short human-readable summary file ./artifacts/product_summary.md (max 2 pages) that lists top 10 features, high-level architecture notes (languages, frameworks, major modules), current CLI/UX surface, and any existing roadmap/ISSUES/FEATURE files found.
- While scanning, extract any existing tests, CI scripts, and build/deploy scripts and put a list into ./artifacts/product_ci_cd.json.

Requirements and implementation notes:
- Use static analysis of code, README(s), package manifests (package.json, pyproject.toml, go.mod, Cargo.toml, setup.cfg, etc.), Dockerfiles, and any docs/ or README files.
- If the repository contains multiple subprojects, enumerate them separately in the JSON.
- Include file paths and short code snippets (1-3 lines) as evidence for each feature when possible.
- Do not attempt to write the final recommendations or implementation plan in this task. Save all outputs in the artifacts/ directory.

You are part of a swarm team. Complete this specific task. When done, use `team_task_complete` to mark task "s1" as completed.
Focus only on your assigned task — other teammates are handling other parts.

members.name: swarm-s2
members.spawn_prompt:
## Swarm Task: Competitor code & feature extraction

**Task ID:** s2
**Title:** Competitor code & feature extraction

Scan the competitor codebases and repos to extract features, UX elements, and notable implementation approaches. Repositories to analyze (relative paths): ../claude-code-sourcemap, ../opencode. Also research high-level behavior of copilot-cli and roocode (if local code is not available, analyze their public docs, READMEs, release notes).

Produce these outputs in ./artifacts:
- ./artifacts/competitors_features.json: array of features per competitor. Each entry: competitor_name, feature id, title, description, where implemented (file paths or docs), UX notes (commands, flags, UI screens), architecture notes (language/framework), and any unique selling points.
- ./artifacts/competitor_comparison_table.csv: CSV comparing feature rows across competitors and columns for presence/absence, maturity, and notes.
- ./artifacts/competitor_highlights.md: short writeup (1 page) summarizing the top 8 features across all competitors that are likely relevant or differentiating.

Requirements and implementation notes:
- For ../claude-code-sourcemap and ../opencode, perform the same static analysis approach as Task s1: manifest files, README, CLI entrypoints, important modules.
- For copilot-cli and roocode, if no local source is present, fetch README and official docs (no external network if agent cannot; in that case, rely on any available local docs and clearly note that remote docs were not fetched). Document any assumptions.
- Save all outputs in the artifacts/ directory with clear filenames.

You are part of a swarm team. Complete this specific task. When done, use `team_task_complete` to mark task "s2" as completed.
Focus only on your assigned task — other teammates are handling other parts.

members.name: swarm-s3
members.spawn_prompt:
## Swarm Task: Identify missing features and prioritized recommendations

**Task ID:** s3
**Title:** Identify missing features and prioritized recommendations

Synthesize the product inventory (./artifacts/product_features.json and product_summary.md) and competitor outputs (./artifacts/competitors_features.json and competitor_highlights.md) to identify feature gaps and opportunities.

Produce these artifacts:
- ./artifacts/missing_features.json: array of proposed feature additions. Each entry must include: id, title, detailed description, reason (competitor parity, UX gap, technical debt, performance, security), evidence linking back to product feature ids and competitor feature ids (file paths or README lines), estimated priority (P0/P1/P2/P3), rough complexity estimate (Small/Medium/Large), required components (frontend/backend/cli/infra/ML), and suggested owner/team.
- ./artifacts/feature_rationale.md: for each proposed feature, a 1-3 paragraph rationale explaining user value, competitor precedent, and technical feasibility.
- A prioritized top-10 list of features to implement first (in the JSON and MD).

Constraints and notes:
- Prioritize features by user value vs implementation cost. Use simple heuristic: Priority = (Value score 1-5) / (Effort score 1-5) and map to P0..P3.
- Make the output deterministic and reference exact evidence from earlier artifacts (file paths, code sections, README lines).

You are part of a swarm team. Complete this specific task. When done, use `team_task_complete` to mark task "s3" as completed.
Focus only on your assigned task — other teammates are handling other parts.

members.name: swarm-s4
members.spawn_prompt:
## Swarm Task: Implementation plan: milestones, tasks, estimates and risks

**Task ID:** s4
**Title:** Implementation plan: milestones, tasks, estimates and risks

Create a concrete implementation plan for the prioritized features from ./artifacts/missing_features.json. Produce a milestone-driven plan with tasks, time estimates, dependencies, and risks.

Deliverables (saved to ./artifacts):
- ./artifacts/implementation_plan.json: structured plan containing an ordered list of milestones. Each milestone must include: id, title, goal summary, start/end estimate (sprint count or calendar weeks), list of tasks. Each task must include: id, title, description, estimated effort in person-days, required skills (frontend/backend/ml/devops), dependencies (task ids within this plan), acceptance criteria (clear testable criteria), and risk level (low/medium/high).
- ./artifacts/implementation_timeline.md: a human-readable timeline (Gantt-style summary) mapping milestones to sprints/weeks and indicating parallelizability.
- ./artifacts/resource_and_risk.md: suggested minimal staffing (roles and FTE estimates), major technical risks for each milestone, mitigation suggestions, and approximate cost drivers.

Constraints and notes:
- Use the prioritized top-10 features from Task s3. For each feature, break down into implementation tasks granular enough to allow different agents/team members to work in parallel (e.g., API design, backend implementation, DB migration, CLI integration, frontend UI, e2e tests, docs).
- Minimize cross-task dependencies where possible; explicitly declare dependencies inside implementation_plan.json.
- Provide conservative and optimistic estimates for each task (low/high).

You are part of a swarm team. Complete this specific task. When done, use `team_task_complete` to mark task "s4" as completed.
Focus only on your assigned task — other teammates are handling other parts.

members.name: swarm-s5
members.spawn_prompt:
## Swarm Task: Assemble SWARMOUT2.md final document

**Task ID:** s5
**Title:** Assemble SWARMOUT2.md final document

Consume the synthesized outputs and plans to produce the final document SWARMOUT2.md at the repository root. The doc must be polished and structured for stakeholders. Required sections:

- Title, authorship (automatically note which artifacts were used), and date.
- Executive summary: 3-5 bullet points summarizing recommended feature additions and overall plan.
- Product summary (from s1): 1-page high-level description of product features and architecture.
- Competitive analysis (from s2): concise comparison and key competitor highlights.
- Missing features and recommendations (from s3): for each proposed feature, include the description, rationale, priority, complexity, and direct evidence links (file paths). Include the top-10 prioritized list prominently.
- Implementation plan (from s4): milestones and condensed tasks table with timelines and acceptance criteria. Include a simple Gantt/summary chart ASCII or markdown table.
- Risks, resource estimates, and next steps.
- Appendices: links to artifact JSON files, comparison CSV, and any important code snippets.

Formatting and quality requirements:
- Document must be readable by product managers and engineers. Use clear headings, numbered lists for milestones/tasks, and include tables where useful.
- Ensure every claim references evidence from artifacts by including relative file paths and snippet quotes.
- Save final file at ./SWARMOUT2.md and also save a JSON manifest ./artifacts/SWARMOUT2_manifest.json that lists which artifact files were included and their paths.

Dependencies:
- This task depends on s1, s2, s3, and s4 and must fail clearly if any of the required artifact files are missing.

You are part of a swarm team. Complete this specific task. When done, use `team_task_complete` to mark task "s5" as completed.
Focus only on your assigned task — other teammates are handling other parts.
